purpose: Define the operator-review layer that a Hermes-like collection agent can use without performing legal filing, debtor contact, payment demand, seizure initiation, ledger mutation, or production storage mutation.

operator_review_states.meaning: Machine-generated recommendation has not yet been reviewed by an operator.
Operator or rule contract requires more source-backed facts before any recommendation can be acted on.
Operator accepts the advisory packet for internal review queueing only; it is not execution approval.
A human legal reviewer must inspect source refs, source version, and action packet boundaries.
Operator rejected the recommendation or packet. Rejection is recorded as evidence and no execution may occur.
The case is blocked by StopGate, source uncertainty, debtor-protection, privacy, insolvency, or execution-boundary policy.
No action packet should proceed. This closes the review loop without external execution.

source_ref_rules: Every operator decision must include at least one source_ref unless the decision_code is reject_due_to_missing_provenance.
Source refs must be pointers or curated ids, not raw document bodies.
If a source has review_status other than approved_static_v1, the review_status must be needs_more_evidence, escalated_to_legal_review, rejected, or blocked_by_policy.
Future-effective legal sources may not clear a route for the current evaluation date.
acceptance_checks_for_next_implementatio: Reject any review record missing source_refs unless the reason is missing provenance.
Reject or block any record containing forbidden payload fields.
Ensure review approval never changes direct_execution_allowed to true.
Ensure rejected or blocked review records are retained as audit evidence.
Ensure MCP outputs summarize review records without echoing reviewer secrets, raw OCR text, local paths, or debtor contact payloads.
